decisions.rationale: Finding confirmed: ChatWindow.tsx still uses ai-elements PromptInput and does not mount ChatReusable. However MOD-DRSAM-CHAT decompose CHAT-03 scope explicitly owns mounting DS-06 ChatReusable at bTIUt and lists ChatWindow.tsx as its target_files; DS-06 scope/ACs require delivering and exporting the bTIRW composable shell (ChatReusable, ChatComposer, ChatFloatingDisclaimer), not live route integration. All criteria_audit ACs pass, coverage 100%, and CHAT-03 depends_on is satisfiable via frontend/src/components/chat/index.ts exports. DS-06 target_files overlap with ChatWindow is a decompose handoff point — DS-05 deferred shell wiring expecting DS-06 delivery of composables; runtime mount belongs to CHAT-03 per chat-module decompose.

IR re-read confirms bTIUh parent_id is bTIRW (sibling to header/messages/composer). React nests disclaimer inside a relative composer wrapper to anchor absolute bottom-full overlay — visual overlay intent and z-index/collapse_when_hidden behavior match IR per review ui_evidence pass. AC-2 scopes positioning semantics, not strict DOM parent_id parity; AC-3 requires vertical panel hierarchy which is preserved. Relocating bTIUh to a bTIRW-level sibling would be optional polish with no AC or named ui_ref blocker.

Mode quick-prompt buttons correctly render IR copy and expose onModeSelect callback on ChatComposer/ChatReusable — appropriate design-system composable contract. CHAT-05 decompose scope states coaching mode button UI is owned by DS-06 while this item wires emitted mode-change events to chatconversation context updates. Inert chip behavior until a consumer connects is expected for shell primitives; no AC mandates Bubble workflow prefill/send on chip click.

summary: 0 fix_now, 2 defer, 1 accept. Advance OK — review ok=true, coverage 100%, 0 blockers, all ACs pass. C-1 deferred to CHAT-03-chat-reusable-panel-mount: ChatWindow/ChatReusable runtime mount owned by chat module. C-2 accepted: bTIUh overlay intent met via relative wrapper; strict IR parent_id parity not in AC scope. C-3 deferred to CHAT-05-chat-messaging-and-create-flow: mode workflow wiring explicitly assigned to messaging API item.
